# ADR-0001: Centralize threshold precedence resolution in `cli._resolve_threshold`

**Status**: accepted (auto-generated by `obs-thr-harden` review on 2026-05-23)
**Author**: `obs-thr-harden` review session
**Related initiative**: [`initiatives/_archive/2026-05-obs-thr-harden/`](../../initiatives/_archive/2026-05-obs-thr-harden/)

## Context

The `obs-thr-harden` M2 milestone fixed a real bug: the
`--max-context-tokens` / `--reserved-output-tokens` argparse defaults
(`200_000` / `8_192`) always shadowed the `--aggressive-thresholds`
preset's `context_tokens=4_000` / `reserved_output_tokens=512`, so 2 of
the 8 preset fields never took effect. Fixing it required a single,
unambiguous precedence rule — **explicit flag > aggressive preset >
built-in default** — that had to hold identically across both the
MockProvider REPL (`cli.py`) and the live-provider REPL
(`openai_cli.py`), because the P9/M5 record commits to "the slash
command surface is identical between MockProvider and live-provider
REPLs." PLAN.md M2 specified resolving this precedence "at the top of
`_run_repl`."

## Decision

Resolution was placed in a new module-level helper
`cli._resolve_threshold(explicit, preset_key, default, *, aggressive)`
called inside the shared `cli._build_repl_loop`, **not** inline at the
top of `_run_repl` as PLAN's prose suggested. The three int flags
(`--max-context-tokens`, `--reserved-output-tokens`, `--max-steps`) now
default to `None` sentinels in both CLIs, and `_resolve_threshold` is
the single source of truth for the three-state decision. Because
`openai_cli._run_openai_repl` already delegates to `_build_repl_loop`,
it inherits identical precedence structurally rather than by duplicated
logic.

This reconciles two PLAN requirements that only fit together when
resolution lives in `_build_repl_loop`: (a) the precedence rule itself,
and (b) the M2 exit-gate test strategy, which asserts effective values
by constructing a loop via `cli._build_repl_loop` directly and reading
internal attributes (`loop._budget.max_tokens`, etc.).

## Consequences

- **Positive** — CLI/openai_cli drift on threshold precedence is
  impossible by construction; the symmetry test
  (`tests/test_openai_cli_repl.py::test_openai_repl_aggressive_budget_matches_cli_sentinel`)
  enforces it and fails loudly on any divergence.
- **Positive** — the 8-field × 3-state precedence matrix
  (`tests/test_repl.py`) can read effective values from a loop built via
  `_build_repl_loop`, exactly matching the runtime path.
- **Positive** — the existing `_DEFAULT_CONTEXT_TOKENS`,
  `_DEFAULT_RESERVED_OUTPUT_TOKENS`, `_DEFAULT_MAX_STEPS` constants were
  reused (DRY) instead of introducing a duplicate
  `_DEFAULT_MAX_CONTEXT_TOKENS`.
- **Negative** — `openai_cli._DEFAULT_MAX_STEPS` is now dead code
  (max-steps resolution flows through cli's constant via
  `_build_repl_loop`). It was left in place to avoid extra churn; safe
  to delete in a future cleanup.
- **Negative** — changing argparse defaults to `None` is a user-visible
  behavioural shift; mitigated by a `--help` line and an M3 `--help`
  snapshot test pinning the wording `preset value applies`.

## Alternatives considered

- **Inline resolution at the top of `_run_repl`** (PLAN.md's literal
  suggestion) — rejected: it would not give `openai_cli` structural
  symmetry (the logic would have to be duplicated and could drift), and
  it would not match the exit-gate matrix test strategy that builds
  loops via `_build_repl_loop`.

## Source

This ADR was auto-generated because **HANDOFF.md Section 2 collectively
recorded ≥2 design-decision divergences from PLAN and at least one was
architectural (a new shared precedence-resolution abstraction
deliberately placed to make CLI drift impossible by construction)**. The
full divergence record is in
`initiatives/_archive/2026-05-obs-thr-harden/HANDOFF.md` Section 2's
per-milestone "design decisions (deviations from PLAN)" subsections,
items: M2 #1 (resolution placed in `_build_repl_loop`), M2 #3 (reused
existing default constants), M3 #1 (`_scan_value` superset).


## Future updates

If a later initiative supersedes this decision:

- **Creating the new ADR is Tier B** (auto-applied by the review session
  when the supersedes trigger fires): write
  `ADR-0002-<reversal-slug>.md` with `**Status**: supersedes ADR-0001`
  and link back to this one.
- **Editing this file's Status line to `superseded by ADR-0002`
  is Tier C** (propose-only — review session writes the suggested diff
  in REVIEW.md "Proposed edits"; a human applies it after confirming
  the supersedes reasoning).

This split avoids the review session silently rewriting historical ADR
status lines without explicit human review.
